title: Under-disclosed data categories
evidence: Policy lists name/email/message only; DB stores 10+ additional PII tables (profiles, MFA, consent_audit_log, security_events, analytics, user_products, product_feedback, role_*, email_send_log)
recommendation: Enumerate every PII category per user-type in policy

title: Missing required Art.13/14 disclosures
evidence: No controller identity, no DPO, no per-category retention, no right-to-complain/portability/restriction/object/Art.22, no legal-basis statement
recommendation: Rewrite policy with full Art.13/14 sections

title: Undisclosed sub-processors & US transfers
evidence: Resend, Mailchimp, Paddle, GitHub, Lovable not named; US transfer mechanism (SCC/DPF) not stated
recommendation: Publish /subprocessors page; reference SCC/DPF in policy; verify Supabase region

title: Stale Last-updated date
evidence: Header reads Dec 17 2024; schema has grown materially since
recommendation: Re-date and version policy on every material change

title: RLS hardening verified
evidence: 17 PII tables tested; all returned HTTP 401 to anon SELECT and INSERT

title: SELECT policies scoped to role public
evidence: Pattern inconsistent with rest of schema (rest scoped to authenticated)

title: Origin allowlist not server-side enforced
evidence: POST from Origin https://evil.example.com to send-contact-email returned HTTP 200; only browsers blocked by ACAO
recommendation: Reject non-allowlisted Origin server-side as track-analytics already does

title: In-memory rate-limit bypassable across isolates
evidence: new Map() is per-isolate; horizontally scaled functions reset state per cold start
recommendation: Move rate-limit state to Postgres with upsert on (ip_hash

title: No CAPTCHA on public POSTs
evidence: Combined with F-08 enables spam relay through dlinrt.eu sender domain
recommendation: Add Cloudflare Turnstile or hCaptcha invisible challenge

title: Account deletion path correctly purges core PII
evidence: Requires password+MFA re-auth; cascades FKs on profiles/user_roles/user_products/mfa_*/consent_audit_log/role_*
recommendation: Extend to scrub email-keyed rows (see F-12)

title: Hashing/signing primitives correct
evidence: Backup codes bcrypt-hashed via store-backup-code; unsubscribe tokens HMAC-SHA256 with 3-day TTL

target: contact_submissions, analytics_*, security_events, email_send_log, newsletter_subscribers, product_feedback
title: No automated retention or post-deletion email scrubbing
evidence: Policy promises 2y/1y retention but no cron purges these tables; delete-account does not scrub email-keyed PII
recommendation: Add scheduled cleanup-retention edge function + email-scrub step in delete-account

title: No self-service data export
evidence: Only manual email path to info@dlinrt.eu
recommendation: Add Download My Data button returning JSON bundle of user-owned rows

title: Column allows raw IP storage
evidence: Column typed text rather than hashed (others use ip_hash); contradicts Core security rule
recommendation: Hash to SHA-256 before insert or rename column and migrate existing rows
